body.title: cc_queue.py — auto-clear gates referencing just-closed ticket id on cmd_done
body.result: _ungate_dependents added to cmd_done — scans pending tickets whose gate references just-closed id and nulls them. Retroactive sweep unstuck 6 tickets including T-cc-walk-02 (now Igor-claimable). 6 new tests pass. Commit 2162fa85.
body.description:
T-cc-walk-01 closed at 02:09 today; T-cc-walk-02 still has gate='T-cc-walk-01-bliss-igorbase' (and the rest of the chain stays gated transitively). cmd_done in cc_queue.py only triggers _decision_rollup — no logic clears gates referencing the just-closed ticket id. Result: any chained gating sticks until manually ungated.

Fix: in cmd_done, after the rollup call, scan all pending tasks; for each task whose gate text contains the just-closed ticket id, null the gate and log the action. Mirrors the existing _decision_rollup ungate pattern at the ticket-id level.

After shipping, manually run on T-cc-walk-01 (since 01 already closed) to retroactively unstick T-cc-walk-02..10.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py + tests/test_cc_queue.py (add test for new behavior).
**Design rules:** none apply (clerical workflow fix).
**Scope boundary:** in scope: scan-and-clear pending gates referencing just-closed ticket id on cmd_done. Out of scope: rewriting gate semantics, retroactive scan of all done tickets (one-shot manual fix afterward).
**Test plan:** unit test that cmd_done clears gates referencing the closed id; existing tests stay green. Verify via cc_queue.py list that T-cc-walk-02 becomes pickable after the manual retro-fix.
